science.title: Asteroid Bennu’s Minerals and Organic Matter Occur in Distinct Chemical Domains: Study
science.description: A new nanoscale analysis of the Bennu sample OREX-800066-3 returned by NASA’s OSIRIS-REx mission shows that organic compounds and minerals cluster into distinct regions, suggesting water once altered the asteroid in uneven, localized ways.

science.title: Cell 'snowball' may be answer to large-scale tissue engineering
science.description: Cell cultures—single layers of cells grown in a small dish—have enabled researchers to study biological growth, develop or test drugs and even discover what causes some diseases. Cell spheroids, 3D versions of cell cultures built using a process known as cell aggregation, are the next step in advancing this work, capable of more closely modeling real tissue. A new technology, invented by researchers from Penn State and detailed in a paper published in Advanced Science, could breathe fresh air into bottom-up tissue fabrication and potentially large-scale tissue engineering by addressing these issues.

science.title: Moon crater named after Artemis commander’s deceased wife
science.description: Mrs Carroll Taylor Wiseman - wife of mission commander Reid Wiseman - died of cancer in 2020. Read more at straitstimes.com.

science.title: Crisis in space as Artemis II toilet breaks leaving astronauts with no other options
science.description: The toilet on board the Artemis II spacecraft has reportedly broken down again, leaving the four-member crew without a working alternative for nearly five days.

science.title: Stitching precise patterns-with lasers
science.description: Just as embroiderers, with needle and thread, can transform plain fabric into an intricate pattern, engineers can use lasers and polymers to create flexible, complex structures that could transform life-saving sensing technology. An interdisciplinary team at the University of Pittsburgh's Swanson School of Engineering has developed a new manufacturing strategy that reveals where and how laser-induced graphene (LIG) forms on polymers.

science.title: A ‘molecular fence’ helps turn carbon dioxide into fuel

ai.title: Tech Firms Take Foot Off Hiring Pedal at AI Turn
ai.description: Tech hiring has taken a hit at a time when companies are shedding staff, ostensibly because artificial intelligence is taking over roles that humans handle. Active openings have fallen 8% to 110,000 this month from 119,000 in March, according to staffing firm Xpheno.
